div1.entryFree:
Shiver, vb. 1) tr. to break into splinters, to dash to pieces: --ed all the beauty of my glass, Lucr. 1763. --ing shocks, Mids. I, 2, 34.
2) intr. a) to fall into pieces: thou'dst --ed like an egg, Lr. IV, 6, 51.
b) to quake, to tremble: I have seen them s. and look pale, Mids. V, 95. --ing cold, R2 V, 1, 77.
